conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me what brought you in today? What's been going on that's got you wanting to talk with someone about this?
Honestly, things just feel really tough lately. I'm having trouble focusing at work and I'm behind on projects already.
That sounds stressful. Can you walk me through your daily routine? How do you usually start your day?
Well, I wake up early, try to get some coffee in me, but then it takes forever to get going. By the time I get to the office, I'm just trying to stay awake.
That sounds like a challenge. What's been going on at work that's got you feeling so drained?
It's just...people are always asking for things and I feel like I'm just not doing enough. Like, I should be more productive or something.
I see. And how has your social life been lately? Have you been spending time with friends or family?
Honestly, it's been tough to keep up with people lately. I've been canceling plans left and right because I just don't have the energy.
That must be frustrating. Can you tell me what you usually do in your free time? Do you have any hobbies or interests that bring you joy?
Yeah, I used to love hiking, but lately, it's been hard to get motivated to even go for a walk.
I understand. Sometimes it feels like we just can't muster up the energy to do things anymore. How has your appetite been? Are you eating okay?
Oh, no, not really. I've just lost interest in food altogether.
That sounds like a real struggle. Have you noticed any physical sensations or body feelings that might be related to how you're feeling?
Honestly, my muscles ache all the time. It feels like I'm always exhausted.
I'm so sorry to hear that. Can you think of anything specific that's happened recently that might have triggered these feelings? Any big changes or stressful events?
Well, my sister got married and I didn't get invited. That really hurt.
Ah, that can be a tough one. How did you feel about not being included in the celebration?
It just felt like nobody wanted me there, you know? Like, I was an afterthought or something.
I understand how that could be really painful. What kind of support system do you have around you right now? Are there any friends or family members who can offer a listening ear?
Actually, my best friend from childhood has been trying to check in on me, but it's hard for me to reach out.
That's great that you have someone looking out for you. What are your goals for our time together? What do you hope to get out of therapy?
Honestly, I just want to feel better. I don't know how much longer I can keep going like this.
That makes sense. And finally, what's one thing that you're looking forward to in the next week or so? Something that might bring a little bit of joy into your life?
I guess I'm looking forward to my favorite TV show coming back on. It's something I always look forward to.
That sounds like a nice escape! Well, our time is just about up for today. Is there anything you'd like to add or any questions you have before we wrap up?
Yeah, actually - how often do people usually come in? Should I be coming back every week or something?
We can discuss that next session. For now, it looks like we're a good fit for weekly sessions. I'll see you same time next week!
Sounds good to me.
Alright! And just one more thing - is there anything in particular that's been getting in the way of your usual daily routine or habits lately?
Yeah, my energy levels are just so low. It feels like it takes forever to get out of bed and I'm always tired by the time I get to work.
Okay, got it! And finally - how's your appetite been handling all this? Have you noticed any changes in that area?
Honestly, my stomach's been growling nonstop. I feel like I need to eat something constantly just to keep from getting hangry!
That sounds like a real struggle. Well, our time is up for today!
